name: Drew Lock
headline: Seattle's QB2 stays a contingency bet behind Darnold
body:
Lock's recent profile reinforces why he's a depth piece, not a fantasy asset. His 2024 sample in New York was the high-water mark: seven games, 1,071 yards, six touchdowns against five picks, but a 41.7% success rate, minus-0.145 EPA per play, and a minus-6.1 CPOE all flagged below-average efficiency. The 2025 cameo in Seattle was three attempts and a 0.1-point line, pure mop-up duty. Across three years his EPA per play has never cleared zero, and the turnover-prone tendencies (four fumbles in 2024 alone) cap any ceiling even in extended action.

The path forward is purely conditional. He's locked in behind Sam Darnold and now fending off Jalen Milroe for the No. 2 job, with an ADP near 389 and essentially no rosters. He matters only in Superflex builds, and only if Darnold misses time. Until then, treat Lock as a waiver-stash insurance policy rather than a draftable name.
